Cell & Gene Therapy Symposium 2026

Advancing the frontiers of curative medicine—where pioneering science seamlessly translates into clinical impact. Join a distinguished global community of researchers, clinicians, and industry leaders as we explore breakthrough innovations, transformative therapies, and the future of translational medicine.

October 9–10, 2026
Christian Medical College, Vellore
100+ Delegates Expected
Why Attend
Two Days of Transformative Science

The 11th Annual Cell and Gene Therapy Symposium brings together over 100 experts from academia, clinical practice, and biotech to explore the latest breakthroughs in curative therapies.

Cutting-Edge Research

Hear directly from principal investigators publishing in leading journals on the latest discoveries shaping the field..

Clinical Translation

Case studies and data from pivotal trials bringing cell and gene therapies from bench to bedside worldwide.

Networking

Connect with 100+ thought leaders, fostering collaborations that accelerate the development of next-generation therapies.

Industry Showcase

Explore innovative platforms, tools, and technologies from leading biotech and pharma companies in the exhibition hall.

About the Symposium
A Decade of Progress.
A Future of Cures.

Now in its 11th year, the Cell and Gene Therapy Symposium has established itself as the premier gathering for CGT professionals across North America and beyond. Founded in 2015 with just 80 attendees, the symposium has grown into a flagship event drawing global expertise in viral vectors, CAR-T cells, gene editing, and advanced manufacturing.

Our mission is to accelerate the path from scientific discovery to life-changing treatments for patients with rare and complex diseases. Through rigorous scientific sessions, collaborative workshops, and meaningful networking, CGT 2026 will continue pushing the boundaries of curative medicine.

11th
Annual Edition

Organizing Committee
Scientific Leadership

The symposium is organized by a dedicated committee of clinician-scientists and translational researchers committed to advancing the CGT field.

Prof. Linda Hartman, PhD
Symposium Chair · MIT
Pioneer in AAV vector engineering and in vivo gene correction strategies.
Dr. Marcus Chen, MD
Co-Chair · Stanford Medicine
Leading clinical trials in hematological malignancies using CAR-T platforms.
Dr. Priya Nair, PhD
Scientific Director · Broad Institute
CRISPR-based therapy development for rare monogenic diseases.
Scientific Programme
Core Themes 2026

This year's symposium is structured around six major scientific themes, each reflecting the most dynamic and clinically impactful areas in cell and gene therapy.

CRISPR & Base Editing

Advances in precision genome editing, base editors, prime editors, and epigenome targeting for monogenic and complex diseases.

CAR-T & Cell Therapies

Next-generation CAR constructs, allogeneic platforms, NK cell therapies, TIL therapy, and overcoming solid tumor resistance.

Viral Vector Delivery

AAV capsid engineering, lentiviral optimization, novel tropism, immune evasion strategies, and scalable GMP manufacturing.

Non-Viral Delivery

Lipid nanoparticles, polymeric nanocarriers, exosome-based delivery, and electroporation advances enabling safer gene transfer.

Regulatory & Manufacturing

FDA and EMA pathways, accelerated approval designations, GMP compliance, cost-of-goods challenges, and commercial-scale production.

Patient Access & Ethics

Health economics, equitable access, informed consent in early-phase trials, germline editing ethics, and global policy frameworks.

Location
Boston Convention & Exhibition Center

Located in the heart of Boston's biotech hub, the BCEC offers world-class facilities for our three-day symposium.

Venue Details

Boston Convention & Exhibition Center
415 Summer Street, Boston, MA 02210

One of the largest convention facilities in New England, the BCEC provides over 516,000 sq ft of exhibition space and state-of-the-art meeting rooms perfectly suited for the symposium.

Getting There

By Air: Boston Logan International Airport (BOS) — 15 min by taxi or Silver Line.

By Transit: Silver Line SL1/SL2 stops directly at the BCEC. Red & Green Line connections available at South Station.

By Car: On-site parking available. I-90 Exit 25 (South Boston).

Boston Convention Center
415 Summer St, Boston, MA
Interactive map available upon registration
